slides.title: Give your worry a *scheduled* time of day.
slides.sub: A daily worry window keeps anxious thoughts in one place instead of all day. Here is why it matters for aging.

slides.title: A daily worry window
slides.body: Set aside *15 quiet minutes* each day to think through what is bothering you. Outside that window, you gently set worries down.

slides.title: Rumination loses its grip
slides.body: When a worry has a time slot, your mind stops *looping on it* all day. The thought waits instead of running on repeat.

slides.title: Less chronic cortisol
slides.body: Constant worry keeps stress hormones high. Contained rumination *can lower* that steady cortisol drip your body never gets to clear.

slides.title: Calmer cells, slower aging
slides.body: Chronic stress *is linked to* shorter telomeres, the caps that protect your DNA. Easing it may help your cells age more slowly.

slides.title: Same time, same chair
slides.body: Pick a fixed time, write the worries down, then close the notebook. The *routine* is what trains your brain to let go.

slides.title: Track the calm you *build*.
slides.body: Vibe Health turns your sleep, food, movement and steps into one daily Health Score, so you can see stress habits add up over time.
slides.cta_text: search ‘Vibe Health’ on the App Store

caption:
Worry less by giving it a time slot. A daily worry window keeps anxious thoughts in one place instead of leaking into your whole day. 🌿

Contained rumination can ease the chronic cortisol that wears on your cells, and lower stress is linked to slower aging. Save this for tomorrow. 🔖

youtube_title: The Daily Worry Window That May Slow Aging
